column1: Here are the updated spaghetti models. Now that we have a center of circulation, the models (and track) should remain rather consistent.

Legend has it that Native American burial mounds are the reason that the area hasn’t been hit by a hurricane since 1921.
According to the legend, the Native American tribe Tocobaga built burial mounds all across the Tampa Bay area, the largest one resting in what is now known as Safety Harbor in Florida, just 30 minutes from Tampa. We have been saved again. 🙌🏼

my great great grandfather homesteaded pass-a-grille and passed this story to my grandfather and father. Along with a much more amusing tale of Jose Gaspars treasure being buried in Tampa Bay and that Jose put a Pirates protection on the bay till he returned for it. Fortunately for us Jose passed before he could return for it so the protection stays.
Either one - I’ll take it.

Native Americans were in touch with nature/the great outdoors; modern man is so out of touch with their environment. I am sure the chosen sacred burial site was chosen to keep those souls safe from weather, etc.... And so a "legend is born......
I am pretty sure the Native Ancestors are pretty disappointed with how we have desecrated their land, so I wouldn’t bank on that protection much at this point.

I’ve heard this a lot. There are several along & in Tampa Bay (like up in along the actual bay). There’s a large one in New Port Richey, too almost at
the end of the ‘Cotee River. ❤️‍🩹🫶

I wish they would have built those in Southwest Florida so then maybe we'd have been avoided being hit by hit by Charley, Wilma, Irma and Ian lol or rather they should have just built those throughout the entire state of Florida so we would have been avoided being hit by the rest of them lol
if you visit Philippe park in safety harbor there is a memorial mound in reference to the legend.
they said that about North Port, FL and we got hit hard by Ian
most likely has to do with the depth of the waters around Tampa.
I think it's just luck, but I like legends.
I adore legends. But I would have to wonder, if it were an ancient burial ground, wouldn't it have protected the area much longer and we wouldn't have had the hurricane in 1921?
